popular_liberalism_4T: Juarista/Reforma lineage claimed as popular-liberal inheritance; the 4T as its authentic continuation
procedural_liberalism: Liberal-constitutional tradition read as the rule-of-law and separation-of-powers heritage
tension_what_is_at_stake: Both claim the liberal tradition; neither can accuse the other of abandoning it. Art. 39 sustains both readings

popular_liberalism_4T: Consent reactivated as popular sovereignty and plebiscitary mandate (Art. 39); electoral pact as mandate for transformation
procedural_liberalism: Constitutional compact (Art. 40, Art. 135) as the binding agreement; procedure as the locus of consent
tension_what_is_at_stake: Both invoke the Agreement of the pact with incompatible readings: mandate-to-transform vs. compact-to-constrain

popular_liberalism_4T: Largely absent at doctrinal level; moral-traditionalist register (Cartilla Moral) borrows its tone
procedural_liberalism: Rawlsian neutrality: law separated from morality
tension_what_is_at_stake: Not a primary axis of conflict; noted where moral-traditionalist tone appears

popular_liberalism_4T: Suppressed: technical/law-bound authority (SCJN, INE, autonomous bodies) recoded as elitist obstacle to the popular will
procedural_liberalism: Dominant: judicial independence, jurisprudencia, autonomous expert oversight as reason institutionalised
tension_what_is_at_stake: The reason component is what the 4T erodes and procedural liberalism defends. Primary site of the conflict

popular_liberalism_4T: Dominant source: presidency as authentic voice of the people sutures the Ehrlichian fracture; mañaneras as daily re-enactment
procedural_liberalism: Formally rejected (rule of law, not of persons); in practice a softer technocratic-leadership version
tension_what_is_at_stake: The Ehrlichian fracture the 4T rhetorically dissolves and procedural liberalism insists must remain visible. Art. 39 sustains both
Omission is principled: Glenn (2014) confirms Mexico's civil-law tradition. The 2024 judicial reform moved the system further from any common-law analogy

popular_liberalism_4T: Dominant: Tradition + Agreement (electoral pact) + Authority. Reason suppressed. High mobilising capacity; low counter-majoritarian resilience
procedural_liberalism: Dominant: Reason + Agreement (constitutional compact). Authority formally rejected. High procedural stability; low popular mobilising energy
tension_what_is_at_stake: Constitutionally undecidable on textual grounds alone: the text contains both profiles. Resolution requires winning the political-legitimacy argument — Art. 135 is the only operative procedural arbiter
